content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 8, 1995

Michael P. Kenny
General Counsel
Air Resources Board
2020 L Street
Post Office Box 2815
Sacramento, California 95812

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-051

Dear Mr. Kenny:

We write in response to your request for advice regarding
application of the "revolving door" provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act")¹ to the Honorable Brian Bilbray.

QUESTION

May Congressman Bilbray appear before the Air Resources Board
(the "ARB") in his capacity as a United States Congressman to
represent his congressional district concerning either legislative
or administrative actions the Board considers?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Congressman Bilbray is not paid to represent any person
before the ARB. Therefore, appearances before the ARB on behalf
of his constituents would not violate the provisions of Government
Code Section 87406.

FACTS

Mr. Bilbray was elected to the United States House of
Representatives in November 1994, but continued to serve as a
member of the ARB until January 1995 when he took office in
Congress.

_______________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-95-051
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Section 87406(d) prohibits a former government official, who held a position which entails the making or participation in the making of decisions which may have a foreseeable and material financial effect on any financial interest, from representing any third party for compensation before any agency for which that official worked or represented in the twelve months preceding his or her separation from state service.

Congressman Bilbary is not compensated to represent third parties before the ARB. His compensation is for representing his constituents before the United States Congress. Accordingly, Congressman Bilbary's appearances before the ARB on behalf of constituents would not be prohibited under Section 87406 unless Congressman Bilbary was specifically compensated for this purpose.

Should you have any further questions please contact the undersigned at (916) 322-5901


Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Daniel E. Muallem
    Counsel, Legal Division
